name: Montana Capital Car Title Loans
description_short: Montana Capital offers car title loans up to $50,000 with same-day funding, no credit check required, and the ability to keep driving your vehicle during repayment.
description_long:
Montana Capital Car Title Loans has operated since 2007 and claims to have helped over 375,000 customers access emergency funds. The company specializes in title loans—a form of secured lending where borrowers use their vehicle's title as collateral while retaining driving privileges. Their business model targets consumers with poor or no credit history who need quick access to cash.

The company offers loan amounts ranging from $100 to $50,000, with primary emphasis on same-day or next-day funding. Their application process is conducted online or in-person, requiring no hard credit check and promising pre-approval within 5 minutes. Borrowers keep their vehicle keys and can continue driving while repaying the loan; the title is returned upon final payment. Montana Capital advertises 24/7 access to title loans and multiple disbursement options including direct deposit or in-person pickup as a bank check (per customer review).

Montana Capital differentiates itself through convenience features: online application, extended hours, rapid processing (claims within 1 hour per reviews), and stated acceptance of bankruptcies. Their Google rating of 4.5 based on 59 reviews reflects positive customer service feedback. Staff are reportedly friendly and responsive to inquiries, with customers noting clear explanations of loan terms and quick resolution times.

Critically, this is a high-cost lending product. Title loans typically carry APRs in the 100-300% range, though specific rates are not disclosed on the website. The business model relies on borrowers' financial desperation and creates significant repayment risk; default can result in vehicle repossession. While the company targets those with "less-than-perfect credit," this accessibility comes at a substantial cost to consumers and should be a last resort after exploring payday alternatives or personal loans.
pros: Same-day or next-day funding (one customer reported cash within 1 hour)
No credit check or credit score impact required
Loan amounts up to $50,000, larger than typical payday loans
Keep your car keys and continue driving during repayment
Fast online pre-approval in under 5 minutes
24/7 access to apply for loans online
Accepts applicants with bankruptcies and bad credit history
Multiple disbursement options (direct deposit or check pickup)
cons: Title loans typically carry 100-300% APR; no rates disclosed on website, preventing rate comparison
Repossession risk if borrower defaults—vehicle can be seized despite continued payment efforts
Collateral-based lending creates cycle risk for financially vulnerable borrowers who cannot repay
One customer noted funds arrive as bank check, not cash, despite marketing language suggesting cash
No mention of flexible payment plans despite claims of 'payment plans that suit your needs'
best_for: Borrowers with urgent cash needs ($500-$2,000) who cannot qualify for traditional personal loans or credit cards
Vehicle owners with substantial equity in paid-off or low-lien cars who have exhausted other options
Consumers facing immediate emergencies (medical bills, evictions, utilities) with no credit history or poor credit
diagnosis: Montana Capital is best for vehicle owners facing immediate cash emergencies who cannot qualify for unsecured personal loans or payday alternatives. The critical caveat is that title loans are high-cost debt (typically 100%+ APR) with repossession risk; borrowers should only pursue this option after exhausting payday alternatives, credit union PALs, or personal loans, as default can result in loss of transportation.
services: In-person title loan application at physical locations
Vehicle title return upon loan payoff
